parsed.subtask_body: Cheapest connectivity for required terminals seems to be Port-Dune(5) plus Dune-Cliff(5) plus a path from Cliff to Ridge.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Enumerate cheapest Steiner trees connecting Port, Dune, Ridge; current standout is a 30-edge, 2-frequency tree via Cape and Bay with total cost 60. One more pass would only need to verify no sub-30 connected tree exists under graph constraints.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Enumerate cheapest Steiner trees connecting Port, Dune, Ridge by exploiting low-cost links around Cliff/Cape/Cove/Mill/Bay.
For each promising tree, compute the minimum frequencies needed on its active villages from the induced interference graph.
Use the objective structure: 15 per frequency, so compare edge savings versus whether the tree forces 2, 3, or more frequencies.
First check if a 2-frequency solution exists; if yes, likely near-optimal because edge costs are all positive.
Then test smallest-edge trees with 3 colors if they save >15 versus best 2-color tree.
Exploit that only active villages matter; avoid activating interference-heavy vertices unless they give major cable savings.
Keep trees acyclic and connected on required terminals; optional Steiner villages only if they reduce total edge cost enough.
Target a concrete feasible answer beating baseline 86, likely in the 40–60 range.
If a very strong candidate appears, stop early; otherwise do one more verification pass against nearby alternatives.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct and compare the cheapest plausible Steiner trees and their induced coloring requirements to produce a strong feasible candidate", "p_solve": 0.78, "time_budget_s": 240}

SUB_1: Cheapest connectivity for required terminals seems to be Port-Dune(5) plus Dune-Cliff(5) plus a path from Cliff to Ridge.
SUB_1: The cheapest Cliff-to-Ridge connection is Cliff-Cape(4)+Cape-Bay? no direct; better via Cove/Grove? But Bay-Ridge 16, Mill-Ridge 5.
SUB_1: Best full tree I found is Port-Dune(5), Dune-Cliff(5), Cliff-Cape(4), Cape-Cove(4), Cove-Grove(9), Grove-Rock(10), Rock-Ridge(20) = 57, but too high.
SUB_1: Better: Port-Dune(5), Dune-Cliff(5), Cliff-Cape(4), Cape-Rock(5), Rock-Ridge(20) = 39.
SUB_1: Active villages there: Port,Dune,Cliff,Cape,Rock,Ridge.
SUB_1: Interference among active villages: Cliff-Port, Cliff-Dune, Dune-Port only. Cape/Pine absent, Grove absent, Mill absent, Harbor absent.
SUB_1: Thus active interference graph is just a triangle on {Port,Dune,Cliff}, so 3 frequencies are necessary and sufficient.
SUB_1: Total for that tree = 39 + 15*3 = 84, already beats baseline 86.
SUB_1: Checked whether any 2-frequency tree exists: impossible if using both Port and Dune directly linked and also Cliff with both, but maybe can avoid Cliff.
SUB_1: A 2-color candidate without Cliff is Port-Dune(5) plus Dune-Cape(9) plus Cape-Bay? plus Bay-Ridge(16) = 30 edge cost using Port,Dune,Cape,Bay,Ridge.
SUB_1: Active interference there is none except Dune-Port, so only 2 frequencies needed.
SUB_1: Total = 30 + 30 = 60, much better.
SUB_1: Verified tree is connected and acyclic on required terminals.
SUB_1: Also found Port-Cove(11)+Cove-Cape(4)+Cape-Dune(9)+Cape-Rock(5)+Rock-Ridge(20)=49 with 2 colors, worse.
SUB_1: Another strong one: Port-Dune(5)+Dune-Cove(7)+Cove-Cape(4)+Cape-Bay? none + Bay-Ridge(16) impossible without Bay link.
SUB_1: Best edge-only lower bound for 2-color trees seems around 30; since 1 color impossible due to Dune-Port interference, total lower bound is 60.
SUB_1: The 30-edge 2-color tree likely optimal or near-optimal.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Dune","Port"],["Cape","Dune"],["Bay","Cape"],["Bay","Ridge"]],"frequencies":{"Port":1,"Dune":2,"Cape":1,"Bay":1,"Ridge":2}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Enumerate cheapest Steiner trees connecting Port, Dune, Ridge; current standout is a 30-edge, 2-frequency tree via Cape and Bay with total cost 60. One more pass would only need to verify no sub-30 connected tree exists under graph constraints.

DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I re-checked the current 30-edge tree and found it is invalid because there is no available cable link Bay-Cape.
SUB_2: So the prior best guess must be replaced.
SUB_2: For 2 frequencies, Port and Dune must differ, and we must avoid activating Cliff (since Port-Dune-Cliff is an interference triangle needing 3).
SUB_2: Cheapest 2-color connection from {Port,Dune} to Ridge without Cliff appears to be:
SUB_2: Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cape(9) + Cape-Bay(?? invalid, absent) + Bay-Ridge(16) invalid route.
SUB_2: Valid alternatives checked:
SUB_2: 1) Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cove(7) + Cove-Rock(9) + Rock-Ridge(20) = 41 edges, 2 colors, total 71.
SUB_2: 2) Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cape(9) + Cape-Rock(5) + Rock-Ridge(20) = 39 edges, 2 colors, total 69.
SUB_2: 3) Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cove(7) + Cove-Bay? absent, so no cheap Bay route.
SUB_2: 4) Port-Cape(14) + Cape-Dune(9) + Cape-Bay? absent + Bay-Ridge(16) invalid.
SUB_2: 5) Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cape(9) + Cape-Cove(4) + Cove-Harbor(4) + Harbor/Grove/Bay to Ridge all exceed 39.
SUB_2: For 3 frequencies, the natural cheap tree is Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cliff(5) + Cliff-Cape(4) + Cape-Bay? absent, so not useful.
SUB_2: Best valid 3-color tree I see is Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cliff(5) + Cliff-Bay? absent; use Bay-Ridge(16) via Bay-Cliff(22) gives 48 edges total.
SUB_2: Another valid 3-color tree: Port-Dune(5) + Dune-Cliff(5) + Cliff-Cape(4) + Cape-Rock(5) + Rock-Ridge(20) = 39 edges, total 84.
SUB_2: Since 3-color penalty is +15 over 2-color, any 3-color tree must save >15 in edges to beat the 69 candidate; none found.
SUB_2: The 39-edge 2-color tree uses active villages {Port,Dune,Cape,Rock,Ridge}.
SUB_2: Active interference pairs there: only Dune/Port. Cape/Pine absent, Grove/Rock absent, Mill/Ridge absent, Cliff absent, Harbor absent.
SUB_2: Therefore 2 frequencies suffice: assign Port=1, Dune=2, Cape=1, Rock=1, Ridge=2.
SUB_2: Tree is connected and acyclic.
SUB_2: Current best total cost is 39 + 30 = 69, clearly beating baseline 86.
